with men in all walks of life.


A vote of thanks was rendered
retiring officers who have made the
fight for the referendum during the
past year. Especial mention was
made of the services rendered by
Henry n. Oerter, who was COM
PEELED to decline reelection on ac-
count of lack of time to attend to
the duties OF the office.


A report of what has been ac-
complished since the organization
of the league was made by F.. Fl.
Walker on behalf of the executive
committee. G. r. Case was pres
ent and told of what had been done
by the last Legislature. He also
stated that the state grange Is
carrying on most effective and
energetic campaign through the
farming districts of the state.


The secretary was instructed to
get in correspondence with the
oranges in this section of the state.
that united efforts could be made
to carry on the campaign.
